content:
# co-op.care — Build a care grid. Plug in.
Tagline: The app that comes with a caregiver.

## BOULDER  ·  2026
Build a care grid.
Plug in.

Neighborhood infrastructure for Boulder — built on a cooperative, attested by a physician, paid in four currencies.

## WHAT IT COSTS TODAY
15,000 → 30,000.

Boulder residents over 65 — today and by 2030.

Source · PHI National 2024 · home-care industry baseline

- $55/hr: what families pay
- $16/hr: what the caregiver takes home
- 77%: annual caregiver turnover
## THE CLAIM
The math doesn't work for anyone.

Not the family. Not the caregiver. Not the agency. And not the mother.

## THE REFRAME
Not an agency. Not an app.
A care grid.

Neighborhood infrastructure — the way we already plug into water, electric, and internet. Boulder doesn't have a care grid yet. co-op.care is the care grid.

## WHAT A TALENT LOOKS LIKE
Ten skill categories. Every neighbor has at least one.

- 01 · Food: Meals · groceries · Sunday dinner for three
- 02 · Transport: Rides · errands · appointments · airport
- 03 · Hands-on care: Bathing · dressing · transfers · meds
- 04 · Movement: Walking buddy · PT support · fall-prevention
- 05 · Housekeeping: Cleaning · laundry · garbage · basic repairs
- 06 · Smart home: Nest · FaceTime · tablets — high-schoolers own this
- 07 · Company: Sitting · listening · reading · cards
- 08 · Clinical: RN · OT · PT · MSW · LPN · CNA
- 09 · Administration: Insurance calls · bills · scheduling · forms
- 10 · Witness: Directive signings · hard conversations · end-of-life
## EVERYONE GETS PAID — JUST DIFFERENTLY
Four currencies.

- CASH · W-2 wages: $26/hr clinical & hands-on. $18/hr non-clinical. Real paychecks, taxes withheld, benefits accrue.
- HSA / FSA · For eligible expenses: Equipment, home modifications, LMN-qualifying care. Letter signed by Josh · $199 · 24 hours.
- CARE TOKENS · Time-bank credit: Earned per hour given. Transferable across families and across generations.
- EQUITY · Patron-member ownership: Ownership in the cooperative itself. Builds over time. Aligns the workforce with the business.
## LAST SATURDAY  ·  BOULDER, CO
Jess moved her father into the house next door.

Nobody had to call a lawyer at 2 AM. Nobody had to drive to Denver. The caregiver on Thursday already works for the cooperative her father now belongs to.

## IF THIS WORKS — AND WE THINK IT WILL
What Boulder gets.

One replicable Boulder model. Other cities can study it. Colorado LCA is a specific legal structure — three other states have it; where it exists, we clone.

- $66K: Avoided facility cost per family, per year
- 200: Boulder families served in Year 1
- 40+: Caregivers as patron-member owners
- 500+: Neighbors offering hours on the Exchange
- 4: Boulder high schools running the badge
## THE ASK
Build infrastructure. Not another app.

Two hundred founding members. Boulder. $499/yr locked for three years. Or just bring what you can do — the grid finds where it fits.
